description_long: This is a two-story log cabin with a dark, multi-hipped roof and prominent overhangs, constructed primarily from horizontal wooden logs. The structure features a large covered porch at the front, supported by thick log columns, and a stone foundation that elevates the building slightly above ground level. Multiple windows of varying sizes are symmetrically distributed across the facades, with some grouped in pairs and others standing alone, framed in dark trim. The main entrance is located at the front, accessible via a set of wide steps leading up to the porch, while a secondary door is visible on the side. The roof includes a central dormer and deep eaves, enhancing the rustic and robust appearance. Architectural details such as exposed log ends and a wraparound porch section contribute to the traditional log cabin aesthetic.
description: A large, two-story log cabin with a dark hipped roof, stone foundation, covered porch, and symmetrically arranged windows. The structure features prominent wooden logs, deep eaves, and rustic architectural details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, wide steps, covered porch, and garage door on the right side.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the central dormer, symmetrical window arrangement, and a small side porch.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the porch steps, log wall texture, and a pair of windows on the upper and lower floors.
description_view_3: Opposite rear view with a prominent central window grouping, visible porch section, and deep roof overhangs.
attribute_roof_type: hip roof with multiple hips, deep overhangs, and a central dormer; constructed with dark roofing material
attribute_window_arrangement: Multiple rectangular windows of medium to large size, symmetrically distributed on all visible facades, with some grouped in pairs and others single; prominent central window grouping on the rear
attribute_door_placement: Main entrance door centered on the front facade beneath the porch, secondary door on the right side, and a garage door on the front right
